_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing analytical studies that produce defensible causal or correlational conclusions, with appropriate documentation of assumptions and limitations.
comments: O*NET math skill is general. WIDAI has extensive applied math skills: AB-S-007 (study design), AB-K-008 (statistical modeling), AI-S-001 (ML implementation). The applied use of mathematics is well-covered across WIDAI; the general mathematical skill is a superset.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Weak similarity (nist band: much more dissimilar than similar). The moderate STS score is consistent with a Superset relationship where the O*NET element covers significantly broader ground than the WIDAI KSA. The shared conceptual territory is real but represents a fraction of the O*NET element's scope, compressing the similarity signal.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8582 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. The P > R pattern is atypical for a Superset relationship — it suggests the KSA may contain specificity that extends beyond the FDE's scope. This warrants attention during human review, though it may reflect domain-specific terminology in the KSA that the FDE covers conceptually without matching at the token level.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (97.18%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is common for Superset pairs where the scope gap is large — the FDE covers broader territory than the KSA, so the NLI model sees topical relatedness without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3139) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.3224). The small gap (0.01) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Minimal lexical overlap (0.00% of tokens shared). The strong contrast between near-zero lexical overlap and the STS semantic score confirms this is a cross-domain relationship — the concepts are related through meaning, not vocabulary. This is expected for O*NET-to-WIDAI comparisons where general occupational language maps to domain-specific data/AI terminology.
